title: Flow CyTOF Using Single Cells From Human Islets
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bsf74","text":"Cytometry by time-of-flight (CyTOF) is an application of mass cytometry using antibodies conjugated to rare heavy metal isotopes combined with mass spectrometry. This technique significantly reduces background signals and spectral overlap often seen with traditional fluorescence methods. This protocol describes a technique to analyze multiple cellular features of human islets in parallel using a defined and optimized antibody cocktail.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":439}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"10apb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7h0rr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7m4cc","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1r5he","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":30,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"tvvb","text":"Date updated: January 11, 2021","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

Transfer handpicked islets (approximately 5,000 IEQs) into   conical tube. Add   of 1xPBS w/o Ca²+, Mg²+ (Rockland, MB-008). Centrifuge for 2 min at RT, 180 xg. Aspirate the supernatant.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":100,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":106,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":62,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":82,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"cl3lq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"76ft4","text":"2. Add   of warm (  ) 0.05% Trypsin (Invitrogen, 25300054) to the islets. Incubate at   for 9 min, or until cells are in single cells. Pipette up and down at t=7 min, 4 min, 2 min, 0 min. Check under microscope towards the end of dissociation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":5,"offset":15,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"b3atv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4icnn","text":"4. Stop the trypsin reaction by adding  of 100% FBS (Hyclone, SH3091003) to the disassembled islets and pass cells through BD FACs tube with strainer top (Corning 352235). Use  of 100% FBS to rinse the tube and pass through the strainer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":6,"offset":39,"length":1},{"key":7,"offset":53,"length":18},{"key":8,"offset":155,"length":14},{"key":9,"offset":176,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ebvgk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"747k0","text":"5. Dump out the supernatant and wash cells with PBS with 10% FBS. Wash the cells with PBS and centrifuge for 4 min, 400 xg. Dump out the supernatant.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"39mp3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c8tim","text":"8. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":11,"offset":77,"length":15}],"data":{}},{"key":"16qb2","text":"Note: Time has to be exact since with time, cisplatin will enter live cell membranes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"doc7c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"884ml","text":"10. steps: Thaw the cells in 37 °C quickly, and immediately add the Perm buffer Remove the barcodes ( Fluidigm 201060 ) from -20 °C freezer and allow them to warm up to RT for at least 10 min.
Wash cells 2x with Foxp3 perm buffer ( eBioscience, 00-5523-00 ), 700 xg (2900 rpm), 5min. Note: centrifuge at higher speed after fixation.
Resuspend each sample to be barcoded in 800 µL of perm buffer (aiming at 1 ~ 3x106 cells)
Resuspend barcodes completely in 100 µL of perm buffer and transfer them to the appropriate samples. Mix the samples immediately and completely (pipette immediately).
Centrifuge cells, wash twice with 2 mL of perm buffer. 700 xg. 5 min.
Intracellular stain with master mix in perm +1% FBS at 4 °C overnight. Master mix is 100ul/million for each tube. Master Mix:
Wash with 2x perm buffer at 700 xg, 5min.
DNA intercalator Iridium ( FLuidigm 201192B , aliquoted in pcr tubes) at 1:4000 at RT for 1h in buffer to stain DNA is 2% PFA in PBS with Iridium.
Right before running samples on CyTOF2, wash (2x) with MilliQ H2O (700 xg, 5min)
Resuspend in MilliQ H2O. Adjust cell concentration to about 5x105/ml (expect lose 40% of the starting cells at this step) alternatively count and resuspend to desired concentration.
Analyze on CyTOF at about 300-500 evt/sec
creator: Lili Liang
